name: geofencing
description: Shared point-in-polygon primitive every check-in / clock-in feature depends on. Owns the audited `geofence_checks` log (immutable, insert-only, `gfc_` prefix), the polygon + radius evaluator, the override flow, the mobile pre-flight endpoint (no persistence), and the geometry columns on `branches` + `facilities` (from the future facilities module). Consuming modules never run their own point-in-polygon math — they call `GeofenceService::evaluate($branchId, $lat, $lng, $accuracyM, $subject)` and stamp the returned check id on their own entity. Fires `GeofenceEvaluated` events that consuming modules subscribe to via a `subject_type` filter. Depends on PostGIS (enabled by our own migration `enable_postgis_extension`) + `clickbar/laravel-magellan` for spatial column types + `spinen/laravel-geometry` for runtime geometry math (with a hand-rolled ray-cast + haversine fallback so the module works without the geoPHP classmap in tests).
optional_dependencies_note: Facilities module (when available) owns the `branches` + `facilities` tables that carry the geometry columns this module reads. Until Facilities lands, the branch_id column on geofence_checks has NO FK constraint (documented in the migration). Geography (when available) provides the geolocate service used by the mobile preflight controller. Notifications propagates the override-requested + override-applied notification categories.

spatial_types.rationale: PostGIS spatial column types (POINT, POLYGON, GEOGRAPHY) on Eloquent models.

geometry_math.rationale: Runtime geometry primitives (point-in-polygon, distance) wrapping geoPHP. Our PolygonEvaluator delegates to it in production + falls back to hand-rolled math when the geoPHP classmap is not loaded (tests).

reference_data.rationale: Countries / cities / timezones for address normalisation — NEVER used for geofence math, only for surface metadata around evaluations.

workspace.description: Workspace-facing preflight + override request + check log read.

requires_postgis_note: The `enable_postgis_extension` migration runs on the central Postgres DB (single-database row-level workspaces — no per-workspace hook needed). Non-Postgres drivers (SQLite in unit tests) short-circuit the extension migration so the module continues to boot; spatial columns fall back to plain `geometry` column type which SQLite tolerates syntactically.
